summary: The formal_conjectures dataset contains 4,557 problems (2,571 formalized conjectures in Lean 4, with 1,029 open 'sorry'-marked problems) spanning number theory, combinatorics, analysis, and algebra. Current tracked activity is concentrated in quarterly buckets q1 through q10, each showing 100 issues, while problem fc0000 has a single resolved item. Activity appears evenly distributed across the q-series with limited per-problem detail available, suggesting reporting is aggregated rather than granular.
problems: fc0000: 1 resolved issue (Proof: getCategoryStatsMarkdown), 0 currently open
q1 through q10 each carry 100 open issues, indicating large undifferentiated backlogs per bucket
Aggregate reporting obscures which individual conjectures are nearest to resolution
1,029 open 'sorry'-marked Lean problems remain unproven across all categories
highlights: fc0000 demonstrates a complete resolution workflow, providing a proven template for closing issues
Evenly balanced 100-issue load across q1-q10 suggests consistent, manageable batching
Lean 4 formalization enables machine-checkable proofs, lowering verification risk for resolved items
Clear separation between resolved (fc0000) and open conjectures supports prioritization

suggested_todos.title: Break down the 100-issue q1-q10 buckets into per-problem granularity to surface near-complete proofs
Audit the 1,029 'sorry'-marked open problems and tag low-difficulty candidates for quick resolution
Replicate the fc0000 resolution workflow as a documented template for other proofs
Identify cross-category dependencies to cluster related conjectures for batch proving
Establish per-problem activity metrics so traction can be measured beyond bucket totals
